<insights>

- High coupling (0.75) — 8 imports target "rex-prd-engine"
- Cohesion 0.25 and coupling 0.75 place this in the most fragile satellite tier — the fix command handler and core fix utility have almost no internal relationship beyond being co-located.
- The core/fix.ts utility sits outside the cli/ directory boundary; if it has no other consumers it violates the two-consumer rule and should be absorbed into fix.ts or promoted to rex-prd-engine.
- All four files (2 source, 2 tests) form a clean paired structure — production logic and test coverage are balanced, which is a positive signal for maintenance.
- Cohesion 0.25 and coupling 0.75 are the worst metrics in this batch — active governance required; evaluate whether core/fix.ts deserves its own module or should be inlined into the command handler.
- core/fix.ts living outside the cli/ subdirectory blurs the satellite zone boundary defined in CLAUDE.md; if it has a single consumer (fix.ts), it violates the two-consumer rule and should be co-located or promoted.
- Source-to-test ratio is 1:1 (2 source files, 2 test files) — full unit test pairing is a positive quality signal for a small satellite zone.
- prd-fix-command is the only satellite zone where a non-CLI file (core/fix.ts) lives outside the cli/ directory boundary while still being classified in the zone — unlike chunked-review's batch-types.ts which is a shared contract, core/fix.ts has no documented second consumer, making it a clear two-consumer rule violation.
- The 1 return import from rex-prd-engine into prd-fix-command (fix → rex-unit: 7 outbound, rex-unit → fix: 1 inbound implied) suggests core/fix.ts is the return edge source — the main engine depends on a utility that was split off into a satellite rather than kept in the domain layer.
- core/fix.ts sits outside cli/ but inside the prd-fix-command zone boundary and likely supplies the 1 return import that rex-prd-engine emits back — if core/fix.ts has only one consumer (fix.ts), it should be absorbed into the command handler, which would also sever the return edge and eliminate the cycle.
- The fix satellite zone is the smallest zone (4 files) producing a return edge into the domain engine — the structural remedy is simpler here than in chunked-review: inlining core/fix.ts into fix.ts would close the cycle and collapse the zone to 3 files without losing any behavior.
- core/fix.ts living outside the cli/ directory boundary is invisible to path-based zone governance — a future engineer can add imports to core/fix.ts from non-CLI modules without triggering any governance alert, silently eroding the satellite zone's CLI-only content policy over time.
- Inlining core/fix.ts into cli/commands/fix.ts would simultaneously: (1) eliminate the two-consumer rule violation, (2) sever the 1 return edge from rex-prd-engine, (3) collapse the zone to 2 files, and (4) make the zone's full scope visible from a single file — four structural improvements from one code change with zero behavior change.
- core/fix.ts has a single documented consumer (cli/commands/fix.ts) and lives outside the cli/ directory boundary, violating both the two-consumer rule and the satellite zone CLI-only content policy simultaneously. Inlining it into fix.ts severs the reverse edge into rex-prd-engine and closes both violations in one operation.
- prd-fix-command's core/fix.ts sits in packages/rex/src/core/ alongside keywords.ts and verify.ts (rex-core-utilities zone members) — two different satellite zones both have files classified under their zone while physically located in packages/rex/src/core/. This shared directory between two satellite zones means a developer adding a file to src/core/ cannot determine zone ownership from the path alone, and the zone detection algorithm may reassign files between these satellites across analysis runs.
- packages/rex/src/core/ hosts files from both prd-fix-command (core/fix.ts) and rex-core-utilities (keywords.ts, verify.ts) — two satellite zones sharing a physical directory is an ownership collision; inlining core/fix.ts into fix.ts (already the recommended structural fix) would also resolve the directory ambiguity and leave src/core/ exclusively owned by rex-core-utilities.
- [call graph] 98 internal calls, 38 outgoing, 1 incoming (cohesion: 0.72, coupling: 0.28)
